title: Basic CPU Operational Cycle (Fetch-Decode-Execute)
description: A flowchart illustrating the fundamental cycle a CPU follows to process a single instruction, showing the flow of data and control between key hardware components.
relationships: The PC holds the address of the next instruction, which is copied to the MAR. The MAR is used to fetch the instruction from Main Memory into the MDR/IR. The Control Unit decodes the instruction in the IR. The CU then sends control signals to the ALU and registers to execute the operation (e.g., fetch operands, perform calculation in ALU, store result in ACC). The PC is then incremented to point to the next instruction.
